title: The Edmonton Oilers prepare for the WCF + Louie DeBrusk
summary: The Western Conference Final is officially set, and the Edmonton Oilers are just four wins away from a trip to the Stanley Cup Final. On today’s edition of Oilersnation Everyday, we preview the upcoming series between the Oilers and the Dallas Stars, which is set to begin Wednesday night in Texas. The Oilers are riding high after dispatching the Vegas Golden Knights in five games, while the Stars are coming off a tough six-game battle with the Winnipeg Jets. We'll continue to dive into the biggest storylines heading into this highly anticipated series, including the possible return of Mattias Ekholm to the Oilers' lineup, potential line combination changes from Kris Knoblauch, and whether the depth can continue to shine. We'll also look at the other side, where Roope Hintz and Mikko Rantanen have powered the Stars' offense and a stout Dallas blueline led by Miro Heiskanen will present a new challenge for Connor McDavid and company. We'll break it all down with our usual features: our lineup report, Sherwood Ford Giant Question, one big take, and our playoff report — which also includes a look at the Eastern Conference Final between the Florida Panthers and Carolina Hurricanes, set to begin tonight. Joining us on the show is Sportsnet commentator Louie DeBrusk, who will give his insight on the series and share what he thinks will be key to victory for both clubs. Don't be surprised if former NHL goalie Devan Dubnyk drops in to offer a goaltender's perspective on the matchup between Jake Oettinger and whoever gets the nod for Edmonton. All that and more coming your way on this playoff edition of Oilersnation Everyday! title: Oilers-Stars WCF preview + Maple Leafs humiliated
summary: It’s a special May Long Weekend edition of Oilersnation Everyday! While it’s a shorter episode, there’s still a ton to get into after a wild weekend of Stanley Cup Playoffs action. The Western Conference Final is officially set, as the Dallas Stars eliminated the Winnipeg Jets in six games to book a rematch with the Edmonton Oilers. We’ll dive into the key takeaways from that series, look at how both teams are currently constructed, and assess how the Oilers stack up against a deep, structured Stars squad. Are the Oilers better equipped to handle Dallas this year? Over in the East, Game 7 between the Toronto Maple Leafs and Florida Panthers turned into an absolute blowout. The Panthers steamrolled Toronto with a 6-1 win, ending another disappointing playoff run for the Leafs and advancing to their third straight Conference Final. We’ll talk about what went so wrong for Toronto and just how scary this Panthers team looks heading into the next round. No guests today, just a good ol’ fashioned holiday show with playoff takes, analysis, and some fun ahead of the Oilers’ next big test. title: West Coast's First Win & Freo's Giant Statement
summary: What a weekend for WA Footy! We celebrate the West Coast Eagles finally getting their first win and break down Fremantle's impressive away victory over GWS. Join us as we wrap up all the key results and talking points from a massive Round 10. Just before we go – we know footy brings out a lot of passion, and sometimes those emotions can be a bit much. It's a wild ride, and looking after your head is just as important as anything else. If you're finding things tough right now, please know that support is available, and it's okay to reach out. Here are some places you can connect with to get help and support: Beyond Blue: 1300 22 4636 or visit beyondblue.org.au – They provide support for anxiety, depression, and suicide prevention.Lifeline: 13 11 14 or visit lifeline.org.au – Available 24/7 for crisis support.MensLine Australia: 1300 78 99 78 or visit mensline.org.au – A dedicated service for men.Black Dog Institute: blackdoginstitute.org.au – Provides evidence-based information and resources on mental health. Reaching out is a sign of strength. Look after yourselves and each other.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Could Mattias Ekholm return for the Edmonton Oilers? + Jason Demers
summary: The Edmonton Oilers are officially halfway to the ultimate goal. With two playoff rounds down and two still to go, it’s the perfect time to pause and reflect on what’s been an unforgettable postseason journey so far. From early stumbles to dominant performances, emotional highs and gut-wrenching lows, the Oilers have ridden the full rollercoaster—but have come out of it looking like a team hitting their stride at exactly the right time. On today’s episode of Oilersnation Everyday, we’ll take a step back and evaluate everything that’s brought the Oilers to this point. How has their game evolved since the puck dropped on Round 1? What’s been the biggest difference in their structure, and where have the key turning points been? We’ll also look ahead to Round 3, discussing potential lineup combinations, the injury status of Mattias Ekholm, and the looming goaltending decision between Calvin Pickard and Stuart Skinner. We’ll deliver our One Big Take, ask the Sherwood Ford Giant Question, and go around the rest of the league with our NHL Playoff Report as the Final Four begins to take shape. To wrap things up, we’ll serve up some weekend picks with help from our friends at Bet365. The Oilers are heating up—and if the second half of the playoffs is anything like the first, we’re in for a wild ride. title: The Edmonton Oilers are headed back to the Western Conference Finals
summary: The Edmonton Oilers are heading back to the Western Conference Final—again. It only took five games to dispatch the Vegas Golden Knights, as the Oilers shut out them out 1-0 in an intense, defensive Game 5 showdown. It was playoff hockey at its tightest, with both teams playing a conservative, high-pressure game from puck drop to the final buzzer. But it was the Oilers who found the breakthrough. After 60 minutes of scoreless hockey, the game headed to overtime where Kasperi Kapanen played the hero. The veteran winger crashed the crease and banged home a loose puck at the side of the net, giving the Oilers the win and officially punching their ticket to the Western Conference Final for the third time in the last four seasons. It was another masterclass from Stuart Skinner, who recorded his second straight shutout—stopping all 24 shots he faced. He now sits at 127 consecutive minutes of shutout hockey and is peaking at the right time. On today’s episode of Oilersnation Everyday, we’ll break down everything from Game 5. From our full scoring summary to the biggest moments of the night, we’ll take you through how the Oilers got it done. We’ll give you our One Big Take, tackle the Sherwood Ford Giant Question, and go around the rest of the NHL as the second round winds down and we wait to see who Edmonton’s next opponent will be. The job isn’t finished, but the Oilers have made a major statement—and the city of Edmonton is buzzing. Let’s talk about it. title: The Edmonton Oilers are 1 win away from the WCF + Bruce Curlock
summary: The Edmonton Oilers are staring down a massive opportunity tonight in Las Vegas. After dropping the Game 3 of this second-round series, the Oilers delivered one of their best performances of the season, and now have a 3-1 series lead heading into Game 5 at T-Mobile Arena. They’re one win away from advancing to the Western Conference Final—and if their performance in Game 4 is any indication, they’re trending in the right direction. Stuart Skinner silenced critics and delivered his best performance of the postseason in Game 4, stopping all 23 shots he faced to secure his second career playoff shutout. The Oilers’ depth continues to shine, with Adam Henrique scoring twice and Evander Kane chipping in with another gritty goal. It was a statement night from Edmonton, and now they have a chance to finish off the Golden Knights in their own building. But Vegas won’t go quietly—expect a desperate, physical response from a team fighting to keep its season alive. On today’s episode of Oilersnation Everyday, we’ll preview every angle of Game 5. We’ll have our full lineup report, break down key game notes, deliver our Sherwood Ford Giant Question, give you our One Big Take, and check in with the Bet365 Gameday Betting Challenge. Can the Oilers replicate the effort and composure they showed in Game 4? And what kind of storm will they need to weather from Vegas early on? Later in the show, we’ll be joined by Oilersnation writer Bruce Curlock to dig deeper into what’s gone right for the Oilers in this series, how Stuart Skinner has responded under pressure, and whether or not he believes Edmonton will close out the series tonight—or if this thing is heading back to Rogers Place for a Game 6. title: Glen Boss - Kevie Walters Inside Ball
summary: This week on Kevie Walters Inside Ball, Kevie is joined by champion Aussie jockey and all-round legend, Glen Boss.  The two dive into Glen’s incredible journey — from his early days growing up, to his iconic racing career, including that unforgettable partnership with Makybe Diva. Glen also opens up about one of the scariest moments of his life: the fall that left him with a broken neck.  Plus, a few classic yarns from nights out with Kevie, Alfie and the boys that you won’t want to miss.  Another cracking episode with one of Australia’s sporting greats.
